target.title: fix(gateway): emit subagent_ended hook for api.runtime.subagent.run() (#59164)
target.body:
## Summary

- Plugin SDK subagent runs dispatched via `api.runtime.subagent.run()` never reached `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`, so plugins listening on `api.on("subagent_ended", ...)` were silent for every plugin-spawned run. `sessions_spawn` and ACP subagent flows already call `registerSubagentRun` themselves after spawning; the plugin SDK seam now mirrors that pattern after the gateway dispatch in `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run`.
- Registration is best-effort (a registry failure logs and continues so the public SDK call still returns `runId`), is dynamically imported to keep `server-plugins.ts` off the static back-edge of `subagent-announce.runtime.ts`, and uses the existing gateway request scope to derive a requester key from the configured main-session alias (the same default `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` uses when no caller agent session is provided).
- Public `api.runtime.subagent.run()` signature is unchanged. No new SDK exports, no protocol-version bump, no config additions.

## Linked context

Closes #59164

## ClawSweeper P1 follow-up (2026-05-29)

### Session retention fix

ClawSweeper flagged a regression in the original commit: the helper registered runs with `cleanup: "delete"`, which made the registry auto-delete the child session as soon as `subagent_ended` fired. The documented plugin flow is `api.runtime.subagent.run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()`; auto-delete would race the transcript read and break that contract for existing plugins. The follow-up commit switches the default to `cleanup: "keep"` so the child session survives completion. Explicit teardown stays with the plugin via `api.runtime.subagent.deleteSession()` — same readability default as `sessions_spawn`. The hook still fires in `completeSubagentRun` because `expectsCompletionMessage: false` keeps it on the immediate-emit branch; `cleanup` only governs whether the session is deleted afterward (`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:1113-1131`, `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:901-929`).

### Regression test

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` now includes a new test `"preserves the child session so the transcript stays readable until the plugin deletes it"` that exercises the full contract end-to-end against a mocked gateway router: `run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts transcript still readable) → `deleteSession()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts the session is now gone). The existing `cleanup` assertion was updated from `"delete"` to `"keep"`.

### Real plugin harness proof

Standing up a live plugin harness against a running gateway is out of scope for this contributor — I do not have a Crabbox/Testbox lane plus a representative plugin install to drive `api.runtime.subagent.run()` against the patched runtime end-to-end. The new regression test is the strongest signal that the plugin contract (run → wait → read → delete) is preserved on the patched adapter, combined with the existing `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` and `subagent-registry-completion.test.ts` coverage of the registry side.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `subagent_ended` hooks did not fire for runs dispatched through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Root cause confirmed at source: `src/gateway/server-plugins.ts` plugin SDK `run` adapter dispatched to gateway `agent` (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` → `agentCommandFromIngress`) but never created a `SubagentRunRecord`, so `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts`) was never called when the run finished.
- Real environment tested: vitest unit tests inside this worktree, against the patched runtime adapter; no real plugin harness against a running gateway. The clawsweeper:source-repro evidence on #59164 traces the same call graph the test exercises.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` (only pre-existing, unrelated error in `src/secrets/path-utils.ts`)
- Evidence after fix:
  - new test file `src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` — 5 tests pass (includes the new run -> waitForRun -> getSessionMessages -> deleteSession regression)
  - existing `server-plugins.test.ts` + `server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts` — 2 files, 46 tests pass
  - subagent-registry sibling-surface tests — 19 files, 254 tests pass
- Observed result after fix: `api.runtime.subagent.run()` returns the same `{ runId }` as before, AND the registry now holds a `SubagentRunRecord` for the run with `cleanup: "keep"`. When the agent completes, the lifecycle controller calls `emitSubagentEndedHookForRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` and the `subagent_ended` hook fires; the child session stays alive so the plugin can read it back via `getSessionMessages()` and tear it down with `deleteSession()` when ready.
- What was not tested: did not stand up a real plugin harness against a running gateway end-to-end (see "Real plugin harness proof" above). The reporter's source-repro on #59164 plus the registry's existing completion path cover the rest of the journey.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: vitest-only in this worktree; no Crabbox/Testbox run attached.
- Before evidence: clawsweeper:source-repro on #59164 already shows the pre-patch call graph (no `registerSubagentRun` on the plugin SDK path).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` covers:
- `runtime.run()` calls `registerSubagentRun` with the gateway-returned `runId`, the caller's `sessionKey` as `childSessionKey`, the message as `task`, `expectsCompletionMessage: false`, `spawnMode: "run"`, `cleanup: "keep"`, and the main-session alias from cfg as `requesterSessionKey`.
- Plugin scope `pluginId` flows through as a `plugin:<id>` registry label so operators can attribute the run.
- When no gateway context is available (no scope, no fallback), the dispatch throws as before and we do not register a half-baked record.
- A registry exception does not break the public SDK contract — `runtime.run` still resolves with `{ runId }`.
- **New (P1 follow-up):** `run()` -> `waitForRun()` -> `getSessionMessages()` reads the transcript back successfully, then explicit `deleteSession()` makes the transcript unreachable. Regression test for the ClawSweeper finding on auto-delete-after-completion.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the original patch: plugin `api.on("subagent_ended", handler)` handlers never fired for runs started through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Before the follow-up commit on this PR: any plugin that called `getSessionMessages()` after `waitForRun()` would have raced the auto-delete and lost the transcript.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `subagent_ended` hooks now fire for plugin-SDK subagent runs, and the child session is preserved for plugin-side readback until the plugin explicitly deletes it. Both behaviors are the intended contract, not regressions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`. The dispatch still goes through the same gateway `agent` handler with the same `pluginRuntimeOwnerId` plumbing.

What is the highest-risk area?

Double registration on a path that already calls `registerSubagentRun`. Sibling-surface analysis:

- `sessions_spawn` tool (`src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:434`) calls `callSubagentGateway({ method: "agent", ... })`. That `callSubagentGateway` is a session-scoped helper that does **not** route through the plugin SDK adapter, so my new `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly` is unreachable from this path. Sessions_spawn continues to be the only registration site.
- `subagent-spawn.ts:1171` (direct spawn) likewise dispatches via `callSubagentGateway`, not via `createGatewaySubagentRuntime`.
- ACP subagent (`sessions-spawn-tool.ts:419` `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` + `registerSubagentRun`) and Codex subagent paths both register on their own side and do not flow through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`.
- The only caller of `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run` is the public plugin SDK `api.runtime.subagent.run`. So this fix tracks exactly the set of runs that were missing tracking before, with no overlap.

How is that risk mitigated?

`registerSubagentRun` itself is keyed on `runId` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts:481-545`) and a duplicate registration with the same `runId` would only overwrite the entry, not double-emit the hook (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` guards on `entry.endedHookEmittedAt`). The sibling-surface review above shows the duplicate-registration case is not reachable through current callers.

## Current review state

What is the next action? ClawSweeper re-review of the P1 follow-up, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing from the author side. Crabbox/Testbox / real plugin-harness proof can be requested if the source-repro evidence above is not sufficient.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper P1 finding on auto-delete-after-completion — addressed via the `cleanup: "keep"` default change and the new regression test (see "ClawSweeper P1 follow-up" section above).

target.title: fix(agents): surface MCP structured content in tool results
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface inbound bundle-MCP `structuredContent` in model-visible tool output when an MCP server also returns normal text content.
- When `structuredContent` is present, show only the serialized structured block to the model and keep `details.structuredContent` for UI/log consumers.
- Keep the change scoped to the bundle-MCP materialization boundary; provider transports, Codex runtime behavior, and MCP server behavior are intentionally out of scope.
- Also remove an unused local secrets path helper that was failing the latest `check-prod-types` gate on main.

## Linked context

Closes #87511

Related #77024
Related #57461

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. Maintainer follow-up requested that only structured content be shown to the model when `structuredContent` is present.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: MCP servers such as Codex can return `content` plus `structuredContent.threadId`; OpenClaw previously hid the structured fields from the model-facing tool result whenever `content` was non-empty.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with real `codex-cli 0.134.0` / `codex mcp-server`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -`, created a real `createSessionMcpRuntime` with `mcp.servers.codex.command = codex` and `args = ["mcp-server"]`, materialized bundle-MCP tools, called `codex__codex`, extracted `details.structuredContent.threadId`, then called `codex__codex-reply` with that thread id.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted/shape-only console output from the live Codex MCP smoke:

```json
{
  "tools": [
    "codex__codex",
    "codex__codex-reply"
  ],
  "firstResult": {
    "contentBlocks": 1,
    "visibleStructuredContent": true,
    "visibleThreadId": true,
    "detailsThreadId": "019e6f51-516e-7c20-8807-35fa47e24a16"
  },
  "replyResult": {
    "contentBlocks": 1,
    "detailsThreadId": "019e6f51-516e-7c20-8807-35fa47e24a16",
    "sameThread": true
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The first Codex MCP call produced one model-visible `structuredContent:` block containing the thread id, without duplicating the original text block. A real `codex__codex-reply` call then continued on the same thread id.
- What was not tested: Full interactive OpenClaw agent UI/session flow; the smoke exercises the real bundle-MCP runtime, real Codex MCP server, and materialized OpenClaw tool execution boundary directly.
- Before evidence: Current source only serialized `structuredContent` in the empty-content fallback path, so the same Codex-style response previously exposed only the normal text block to the model.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/secrets/path-utils.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` live Codex MCP smoke described above

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added focused bundle-MCP materialization coverage for a tool response that includes both normal text content and structuredContent with a Codex-style `threadId`; updated it to assert the model-visible result contains only the structured block. Also removed an unused secrets path helper to restore the prod-type gate on the latest base.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The model-visible result only included the normal text content, so continuation identifiers stored in `structuredContent` were unavailable to the agent.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Agents can now see serialized structured MCP result fields in tool output.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model-visible result shape changes for MCP tools that return both normal content and structuredContent.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to the existing bundle-MCP result boundary, preserves `details.structuredContent`, and avoids duplicating large normal content plus structured JSON by showing only the structured block when structuredContent is present.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review and CI after the branch update.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer/CI only; live Codex MCP continuation smoke has been provided.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the maintainer/ClawSweeper request to show only structured content to the model when `structuredContent` is present.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code path and ran the focused validation commands above.

commands.author: Gausons

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Preserves compatibility for an existing model alias named `inherit`: aliases resolve before the inheritance sentinel is applied.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior, alias precedence, and config precedence.
- Refreshes the generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, alias compatibility around the `inherit` sentinel, provider-qualified requester model refs, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session, while preserving any configured model alias named `"inherit"`.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `7e88ecd0ba`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`; local loopback Gateway started with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; real provider-backed run on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` using an OAuth auth profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm tsgo:prod
pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
GOMAXPROCS=1 pnpm check:changed
node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order set --provider openai-codex openai-codex:default
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs
node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'
node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json
node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order clear --provider openai-codex
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
passed 2 Vitest shards, 184 tests, in 6.69s

pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

GOMAXPROCS=1 pnpm check:changed
passed
```

Real provider-backed parent run summary:

```json
{
  "runId": "18357622-6740-485f-9adb-44feaea97cd7",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "agentMeta": {
    "sessionId": "417d69e3-4cb7-4775-b71c-ea8fd694077b",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "model": "gpt-5.5",
    "agentHarnessId": "codex"
  },
  "executionTrace": {
    "winnerProvider": "openai-codex",
    "winnerModel": "gpt-5.5",
    "fallbackUsed": false
  },
  "toolSummary": {
    "calls": 2,
    "tools": ["sessions_spawn", "sessions_yield"],
    "failures": 0
  }
}
```

Parent `sessions_spawn` call and accepted result:

```json
{
  "parentToolCall": {
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "taskName": "inherit_model_probe_after_7e88ecd",
      "label": "inherit-model-probe-after-7e88ecd",
      "model": "inherit",
      "context": "isolated",
      "cleanup": "keep",
      "runTimeoutSeconds": 180
    }
  },
  "spawnResult": {
    "status": "accepted",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:b9d31490-5eb8-488f-b836-f4d00c2188f5",
    "runId": "acf2c474-46b2-4e14-a3ca-f31b4e3b37d2",
    "taskName": "inherit_model_probe_after_7e88ecd",
    "modelApplied": true
  }
}
```

Persisted child session metadata:

```json
{
  "key": "agent:main:subagent:b9d31490-5eb8-488f-b836-f4d00c2188f5",
  "sessionId": "1b9eb83a-6844-4398-b518-172bbadb7f37",
  "kind": "spawn-child",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "modelProvider": "openai-codex",
  "providerOverride": "openai-codex",
  "modelOverride": "gpt-5.5",
  "agentRuntime": {
    "id": "codex",
    "source": "implicit"
  }
}
```

Parent and child trajectory model evidence:

```text
parent assistant provider/model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5
child assistant provider/model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5
```

Child assistant message:

```text
CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_7E88ECD_PROBE_OK
Model/provider visible: Codex based on GPT-5.
```

Parent final message after child completion:

```text
Proof summary: child taskName `inherit_model_probe_after_7e88ecd` completed with:

CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_7E88ECD_PROBE_OK
Model/provider visible: Codex based on GPT-5.

Requester model: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.
```

Cleanup verified after the proof:

```text
Gateway port 18789: free
openai-codex auth order: null
```

Observed result: the parent session ran on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, invoked `sessions_spawn` with `model: "inherit"`, the spawn was accepted with `modelApplied: true`, and the child `spawn-child` session persisted and executed with `modelProvider: "openai-codex"` and `model: "gpt-5.5"`. Focused regression tests also verify that a configured alias named `"inherit"` remains a concrete model selection instead of triggering inheritance, and that provider-qualified requester model refs are not duplicated.

What was not tested: external channel delivery was intentionally disabled with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; ACP runtime inheritance remains out of scope for this PR.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof used a local loopback Gateway and a real model provider run, with channel connectors skipped to avoid external delivery. Local `check:changed` was rerun with `GOMAXPROCS=1` after an intermittent `tsgolint` parser SIGSEGV; the rerun passed.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path or alias-compatibility coverage for an alias named `"inherit"`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `GOMAXPROCS=1 pnpm check:changed`
- `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, missing requester model behavior, provider-qualified requester model refs, and a model alias named `"inherit"` winning over the sentinel.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.
- Generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model. The first implementation also treated bare `"inherit"` as a sentinel before alias resolution; this follow-up preserves existing aliases named `"inherit"`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value when no model alias named `"inherit"` exists.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model and alias precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only after alias resolution, preserves provider-qualified requester model refs, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, direct-spawn, prompt-snapshot, and live Gateway seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper to re-run on `7e88ecd0ba`, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The PR body now includes live Gateway proof and alias/provider-qualification compatibility validation for the latest PR head. Waiting on automated checks and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's live Gateway proof request, the provider-qualified inherited model ref finding, the rebase/dirty-branch finding, the prompt snapshot CI drift, and the compatibility finding about a model alias named `"inherit"`.

target.title: feat: add scoped mention pattern policy
target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `ff90f9f9ab47f71bb3054c03186f9f3743f24210`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=ff90f9f9ab47f71bb3054c03186f9f3743f24210 HEAD_SHORT=ff90f9f9ab node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (ff90f9f9ab, ff90f9f9ab47f71bb3054c03186f9f3743f24210)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(agents): surface MCP structured content in tool results
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface inbound bundle-MCP `structuredContent` in model-visible tool output when an MCP server also returns normal text content.
- When `structuredContent` is present, show only the serialized structured block to the model and keep `details.structuredContent` for UI/log consumers.
- Keep the change scoped to the bundle-MCP materialization boundary; provider transports, Codex runtime behavior, and MCP server behavior are intentionally out of scope.
- Also remove an unused local secrets path helper that was failing the latest `check-prod-types` gate on main.

## Linked context

Closes #87511

Related #77024
Related #57461

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. Maintainer follow-up requested that only structured content be shown to the model when `structuredContent` is present.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: MCP servers such as Codex can return `content` plus `structuredContent.threadId`; OpenClaw previously hid the structured fields from the model-facing tool result whenever `content` was non-empty.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with real `codex-cli 0.134.0` / `codex mcp-server`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -`, created a real `createSessionMcpRuntime` with `mcp.servers.codex.command = codex` and `args = ["mcp-server"]`, materialized bundle-MCP tools, called `codex__codex`, extracted `details.structuredContent.threadId`, then called `codex__codex-reply` with that thread id.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted/shape-only console output from the live Codex MCP smoke:

```json
{
  "tools": [
    "codex__codex",
    "codex__codex-reply"
  ],
  "firstResult": {
    "contentBlocks": 1,
    "visibleStructuredContent": true,
    "visibleThreadId": true,
    "detailsThreadId": "019e6f51-516e-7c20-8807-35fa47e24a16"
  },
  "replyResult": {
    "contentBlocks": 1,
    "detailsThreadId": "019e6f51-516e-7c20-8807-35fa47e24a16",
    "sameThread": true
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The first Codex MCP call produced one model-visible `structuredContent:` block containing the thread id, without duplicating the original text block. A real `codex__codex-reply` call then continued on the same thread id.
- What was not tested: Full interactive OpenClaw agent UI/session flow; the smoke exercises the real bundle-MCP runtime, real Codex MCP server, and materialized OpenClaw tool execution boundary directly.
- Before evidence: Current source only serialized `structuredContent` in the empty-content fallback path, so the same Codex-style response previously exposed only the normal text block to the model.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/secrets/path-utils.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` live Codex MCP smoke described above

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added focused bundle-MCP materialization coverage for a tool response that includes both normal text content and structuredContent with a Codex-style `threadId`; updated it to assert the model-visible result contains only the structured block. Also removed an unused secrets path helper to restore the prod-type gate on the latest base.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The model-visible result only included the normal text content, so continuation identifiers stored in `structuredContent` were unavailable to the agent.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Agents can now see serialized structured MCP result fields in tool output.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model-visible result shape changes for MCP tools that return both normal content and structuredContent.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to the existing bundle-MCP result boundary, preserves `details.structuredContent`, and avoids duplicating large normal content plus structured JSON by showing only the structured block when structuredContent is present.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review and CI after the branch update.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer/CI only; live Codex MCP continuation smoke has been provided.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the maintainer/ClawSweeper request to show only structured content to the model when `structuredContent` is present.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code path and ran the focused validation commands above.

commands.author: latensified

target.title: fix(openai): avoid stale Responses message id replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Responses request replay can reuse a signed assistant `message` item id after the corresponding `reasoning` item has been pruned from local history.
- Solution: replay signed assistant message ids only when the immediately preceding replayed item is the paired reasoning item.
- What changed: added a small replay-id policy helper, wired it into the OpenAI Responses transport, and covered the pruned-reasoning case in the existing reasoning replay tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change reasoning-item replay, tool-call replay, payload metadata, model selection, or Telegram formatting.

## Motivation

This prevents a latent OpenAI Responses failure exposed by long-running sessions whose assistant text still has a persisted Responses item id but whose paired reasoning item is no longer replayable. In that state, the next request can be rejected before the model responds.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses rejected replayed conversation history when an assistant `message` item id was included without the required paired `reasoning` item, while existing phase-tagged assistant replay ids still need to survive.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 using the OpenAI Responses transport path for `openai/gpt-5.4`, plus OpenAI Node SDK 6.39.0 in-process Responses loopback against current PR head `a9e4a7b45037a4dda2e745f452a1a2e46f85350b` on May 28, 2026.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'INNER'
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { buildOpenAIResponsesParams } from './src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts';

const model = {
  id: 'gpt-5.4',
  name: 'gpt-5.4',
  api: 'openai-responses',
  provider: 'openai',
  baseUrl: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  reasoning: true,
  input: ['text'],
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
  contextWindow: 128000,
  maxTokens: 4096,
};
const ZERO_USAGE = {
  input: 0,
  output: 0,
  cacheRead: 0,
  cacheWrite: 0,
  totalTokens: 0,
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 0 },
};
const context = {
  systemPrompt: 'system',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'Earlier prompt', timestamp: 1 },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Earlier answer',
          textSignature: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'thinking',
          thinking: 'chain',
          thinkingSignature: JSON.stringify({
            id: 'rs_reasoning_item',
            type: 'reasoning',
            summary: [],
          }),
        },
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Pinned commentary answer',
          textSignature: JSON.stringify({ v: 1, id: 'msg_commentary', phase: 'commentary' }),
        },
      ],
    },
    { role: 'user', content: 'Latest prompt', timestamp: 3 },
  ],
};
const params = buildOpenAIResponsesParams(model, context, {});
const captured = {};
const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: 'test',
  baseURL: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  fetch: async (url, init) => {
    captured.url = String(url);
    captured.body = init?.body ? String(init.body) : '';
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      id: 'resp_test',
      object: 'response',
      created_at: 0,
      status: 'completed',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      output: [],
      parallel_tool_calls: false,
      tools: [],
    }), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
    });
  },
});
await client.responses.create({ ...params, stream: false });
const payload = JSON.stringify(params.input);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  proof: 'openai-responses-sdk-loopback',
  pr_head: 'a9e4a7b45037a4dda2e745f452a1a2e46f85350b',
  request_path: new URL(captured.url).pathname,
  stale_signed_message_id: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
  stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  stale_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_commentary'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_commentary'),
}, null, 2));
INNER
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Before this patch, the live OpenClaw Telegram agent diagnosed a real OpenAI Responses failure after conversation pruning:

```text
OpenAI rejected the next request with:
message was provided without its required reasoning item

Failure shape:
- prior assistant message ids were replayed from textSignature
- paired reasoning items had been pruned/unavailable
```

After this patch, I reran the current-head OpenAI SDK loopback on `a9e4a7b45037a4dda2e745f452a1a2e46f85350b`. It imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, sent that exact payload through OpenAI Node SDK 6.39.0 with an in-process `fetch` loopback, and confirmed that both the OpenClaw payload and the serialized SDK `/v1/responses` request omitted the stale raw signed assistant message id while preserving the existing phase-tagged replay id:

```json
{
  "proof": "openai-responses-sdk-loopback",
  "pr_head": "a9e4a7b45037a4dda2e745f452a1a2e46f85350b",
  "request_path": "/v1/responses",
  "stale_signed_message_id": "msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning",
  "stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": false,
  "stale_id_present_in_sdk_request": false,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": true,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: stale raw signed assistant message ids are omitted when their required reasoning item is absent; phase-tagged signed ids are still replayed, and unsigned assistant messages still get the existing synthetic fallback id.
- What was not tested: a live deployed gateway restart or live OpenAI API request with this exact source branch has not been run; the local focused Vitest wrapper in this worktree also stayed silent and did not finish promptly, so this refresh uses the current-head SDK loopback plus direct TypeScript module import proof instead of reporting a misleading local Vitest pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live OpenClaw agent output above captured the OpenAI rejection text and identified the pruned-reasoning replay shape.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the Responses replay path treated persisted assistant text signatures as independently replayable message ids, but OpenAI can require the corresponding reasoning item to be replayed with that message item.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage checked reasoning replay when reasoning was present; it did not check the pruned-reasoning case where only the signed assistant message survived.
- Contributing context (if known): long-lived sessions can prune or omit reasoning items while retaining assistant text signatures.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a signed assistant message id is omitted when no paired reasoning item was replayed; the same signed id is preserved when paired reasoning was replayed; unsigned text still uses the fallback id.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in request payload construction, before network IO, so the replay-id policy can be tested deterministically.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for the pruned-reasoning signed-message case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None, except affected OpenAI Responses sessions avoid a provider-side 400 when replay history has a stale signed message id without its paired reasoning item.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request replays message id] -> [OpenAI rejects]

After:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request omits stale message id] -> [request remains valid]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS arm64
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest via OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.4`, OpenAI Responses
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram agent session exposed the failure; source regression covers the transport replay policy.
- Relevant config (redacted): OpenAI Responses API model with reasoning enabled.

### Steps

1. Create a replay candidate with a signed assistant text `message` id.
2. Omit the paired reasoning item, matching a pruned history state.
3. Build the next OpenAI Responses request.

### Expected

- The stale signed assistant message id is omitted when paired reasoning is unavailable.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale signed assistant message id was replayed even when the paired reasoning item was unavailable.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: current-head real-SDK in-process Responses loopback via an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof that imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`; whitespace validation via `git diff --check`.
- Edge cases checked: signed id without reasoning is omitted; signed id with reasoning is preserved; unsigned assistant text keeps the synthetic fallback id.
- What you did **not** verify: live gateway deployment on this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: omitting a stale Responses message id may reduce provider-side continuity for that one historical assistant message.
  - Mitigation: the text content and phase metadata are still replayed; the id is omitted only when the paired reasoning item is missing, which is the provider-rejected shape.

target.title: docs(browser): add Notte cloud browser to direct WebSocket CDP providers [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** [Notte](https://www.notte.cc) is a CDP-compatible cloud browser provider in the same category as Browserbase and Browserless, but isn't listed under [`docs/tools/browser.md` → Direct WebSocket CDP providers](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/browser#direct-websocket-cdp-providers). Users have to figure out the config shape themselves.
- **Why it matters:** That section was generically framed in #31085 as *"any WSS-based provider works"*, but until a provider is listed, new users default to the documented options. A short config-snippet documenting Notte unblocks discovery without any code change.
- **What changed:** Adds one `### Notte` subsection right after `### Browserbase` in `docs/tools/browser.md` — single-sentence blurb, JSON5 config snippet, and a Notes bullet list. Mirrors the Browserbase subsection structure exactly.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No source code touched. No tests touched. No new CDP URL shape. Uses the bare-`wss://` root fallback path already supported since #31085 (verified end-to-end against `wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #31085 — `feat(browser): support direct WebSocket CDP URLs` (introduced the WSS-root fallback path this PR documents a new use of)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Notte's WSS CDP endpoint is now documented as a supported provider, mirroring the existing Browserbase listing.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 25.2.0 + OpenClaw `2026.5.7 (eeef486)` from Homebrew, talking to `wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect?token=<NOTTE_API_KEY>`. No local code changes — the documented config dropped straight into `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```
  $ openclaw browser --browser-profile notte open https://example.com
  $ openclaw browser --browser-profile notte screenshot
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  Reproduced today against the live `wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect` endpoint:

  ```
  $ openclaw browser --browser-profile notte open https://example.com
  opened: https://example.com/
  tab: t4
  id: 7FE04AC44931A6E1C799DE4ABF0DC807
  ```

  A screenshot captured via `openclaw browser --browser-profile notte screenshot` against the same session yesterday is a 1254×1111 PNG of the actual rendered `example.com` page (will paste image inline when posting upstream). The local `sharp` optional dep is needed for OpenClaw's screenshot post-processing path, otherwise the documented config drives the live tab end-to-end.

  Playwright `connectOverCDP` flow against the same URL (today, `DEBUG=pw:protocol`):

  | Step | Time |
  |---|---|
  | `connectOverCDP` | 695ms |
  | `context.newCDPSession(page)` | 169ms |
  | `session.send("Target.getTargetInfo")` → targetId | 87ms |
  | `page.goto("https://example.com")` | 631ms |
  | **Total** | **1.8s** |

- **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw drives a real chromium tab through Notte's CDP gateway. `open` returns a real `targetId`. `screenshot` returns a real PNG. Playwright `newCDPSession` completes in <500ms.
- **What was not tested:** Long-running sessions (>10 min), tab-limit enforcement under heavy concurrent open/close, the `attachOnly` mode of OpenClaw's remote-CDP profile. None of these are gated by this docs change.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this PR is not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — this PR is docs-only, and the underlying feature (WSS-root CDP support) is already covered by the tests added in #31085. No new test surface is required.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. Users who don't read the new subsection are unaffected. Users who do can now configure a `notte` profile by copy-pasting the snippet, replacing `<NOTTE_API_KEY>` with their API key.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** — the documented config uses the existing `cdpUrl` field with the existing `?token=` query convention (same shape as Browserbase's `?apiKey=`).
- New/changed network calls? **No** — uses the existing WSS CDP transport path.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.2.0
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw `2026.5.7 (eeef486)` from Homebrew, Node `22.16.0`
- Model/provider: N/A (no LLM involved in the docs change)
- Integration/channel: browser plugin (`extensions/browser`)
- Relevant config (redacted):

  ```json
  {
    "browser": {
      "enabled": true,
      "defaultProfile": "notte",
      "remoteCdpTimeoutMs": 3000,
      "remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs": 5000,
      "profiles": {
        "notte": {
          "cdpUrl": "wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect?token=<redacted>",
          "color": "#7C3AED"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Drop the documented config block into `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (with a real `NOTTE_API_KEY` from `console.notte.cc`).
2. `openclaw browser --browser-profile notte start`
3. `openclaw browser --browser-profile notte open https://example.com`
4. `openclaw browser --browser-profile notte screenshot`

### Expected

- `start` → `🦞 browser [notte] running: true`
- `open` → exits 0 with a non-empty `id` (CDP `targetId`)
- `screenshot` → emits a `MEDIA:` line pointing at a non-empty PNG

### Actual

Matches expected. See "Evidence after fix" above.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (N/A — docs-only, no test was failing)
- [x] Trace/log snippets (included in Real Behavior Proof)
- [x] Screenshot/recording (PNG path captured; will paste image when posting upstream)
- [ ] Perf numbers (N/A)

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** I personally ran each of `openclaw browser --browser-profile notte {start, status, open, screenshot}` against `wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect` and confirmed the documented config works as written. I also independently drove the same endpoint via Playwright's `connectOverCDP` (`context.newCDPSession`, `goto`, `Target.getTargetInfo`) and via raw CDP (`Target.attachToBrowserTarget`, `Target.attachToTarget`, `Browser.getVersion`) to confirm the protocol-level behaviour matches what's documented.
- **Edge cases checked:** Re-runs at +250ms and across multiple fresh sessions returned consistent results; no flake observed in the documented happy path.
- **What I did not verify:** Tab discovery polling under heavy load, the `attachOnly` profile mode, behavior past the Notte free-tier quota.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No** — no schema change, no new required key.
- Migration needed? **No**

## AI-Assisted Disclosure 🤖

This PR was authored with Claude (Opus 4.7). Per CONTRIBUTING.md §AI/Vibe-Coded PRs:

- [x] Marked as AI-assisted in the title (`[AI]` suffix) and description
- [x] Human-run real behavior proof included (see "Real behavior proof" + "Human Verification" above — I personally ran every `openclaw` command against the live `us-prod.notte.cc` endpoint and verified each output)
- [x] Confirmed I understand what the change does (it adds a docs subsection mirroring the existing Browserbase subsection; no source code is touched; no new CDP shape is introduced; the underlying feature was added in #31085)
- [x] `codex review --base origin/main` run locally (Codex CLI `v0.130.0`, model `gpt-5.3-codex`): *"The diff only adds a new documentation section for configuring Notte in `docs/tools/browser.md` and does not introduce code-path changes. I did not identify any actionable correctness issues in the added content."*
- [x] Will resolve / reply to bot review conversations after addressing them

Session prompts / logs available on request.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Notte's documented URL or auth convention changes; users following the snippet hit broken config.
  - **Mitigation:** I'll watch for upstream issue reports on the Notte side and submit a follow-up docs PR if anything moves. The snippet uses the stable `wss://us-prod.notte.cc/sessions/connect?token=…` endpoint that's already used in production.
- **Risk:** Notte service availability outside the documented window.
  - **Mitigation:** Out of scope for OpenClaw — same caveat applies to Browserbase / Browserless.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Preserves compatibility for an existing model alias named `inherit`: aliases resolve before the inheritance sentinel is applied.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior, alias precedence, and config precedence.
- Refreshes the generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, alias compatibility around the `inherit` sentinel, provider-qualified requester model refs, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session, while preserving any configured model alias named `"inherit"`.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `7e88ecd0ba`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`; local loopback Gateway started with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; real provider-backed run on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` using an OAuth auth profile.

```bash
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs
node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'
node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json
node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order clear --provider openai-codex
```

Focused regression tests also verify that a configured alias named `"inherit"` remains a concrete model selection instead of triggering inheritance, and that provider-qualified requester model refs are not duplicated.

What was not tested: external channel delivery was intentionally disabled with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; ACP runtime inheritance remains out of scope for this PR.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof used a local loopback Gateway and a real model provider run, with channel connectors skipped to avoid external delivery. Local `check:changed` was rerun with `GOMAXPROCS=1` after an intermittent `tsgolint` parser SIGSEGV; the rerun passed.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path or alias-compatibility coverage for an alias named `"inherit"`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `GOMAXPROCS=1 pnpm check:changed`
- `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, missing requester model behavior, provider-qualified requester model refs, and a model alias named `"inherit"` winning over the sentinel.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.
- Generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model. The first implementation also treated bare `"inherit"` as a sentinel before alias resolution; this follow-up preserves existing aliases named `"inherit"`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value when no model alias named `"inherit"` exists.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model and alias precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only after alias resolution, preserves provider-qualified requester model refs, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, direct-spawn, prompt-snapshot, and live Gateway seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper to re-run on `7e88ecd0ba`, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The PR body now includes live Gateway proof and alias/provider-qualification compatibility validation for the latest PR head. Waiting on automated checks and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's live Gateway proof request, the provider-qualified inherited model ref finding, the rebase/dirty-branch finding, the prompt snapshot CI drift, and the compatibility finding about a model alias named `"inherit"`.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156625-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Preserve reporter credit for #48360 by RacecarGuy, #62762 by justinschille, and #73535 by DJBlackhawk in the PR body.; Mention #51357 by tianyaqpzm and #42019 by 0xrsydn as earlier closed reports that supplied useful historical evidence.; Do not claim or implement #64400's unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation; say that security-shaped sub-scope is routed separately.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

## Summary

Carry forward the remaining Feishu display-name behavior from #38958 after #51032 already landed the group-name session label fix.

This replacement should stay narrow:
- keep #51032's group-name implementation as the baseline
- add/repair DM display-name fallback through chatMembers with contact fallback

- propagate permission guidance when DM fallback lookup hits a Feishu permission error

Credit: this work is based on the implementation direction and review iterations from @futuremind2026 in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958. The already-merged group-name baseline came from @jnuyao in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51032.
